title: Sabine River Authority Dispute: What Your Officials Aren't Telling You
summary: A growing dispute over water allocation rights along the Sabine River could affect thousands of East Texas residents. Several elected officials have ties to parties involved.
content:
The Sabine River Authority is embroiled in a dispute over water allocation that could affect drinking water supplies for communities across Smith, Gregg, and Harrison counties.

What makes this particularly concerning is that at least two county officials in the affected area have financial ties to entities that stand to benefit from the current allocation framework. These connections were disclosed in their financial statements but have received little public attention.

Water rights have been a defining issue in East Texas for decades, and the outcome of this dispute could set precedent for how resources are managed across the region.

RepWatchr is tracking this developing story and will update the relevant officials' scorecards as votes and actions are taken.
author: RepWatchr Staff
